system_boot_content: AIOS has finished initialization. This is the first entry recorded by the system. Everything that happens after this - conversations, tasks, memories - will settle here.
memory_seed_app_creation_guide_descripti: Wandesk React/TypeScript app structure, registries, APIs, database, language baking, and reload workflow
memory_seed_app_creation_guide_content:
# App Creation Guide

This is long-term memory for AI agents developing Wandesk apps. Before editing, inspect nearby implementations and follow the current React/TypeScript structure instead of old Vue/JS or generic Express habits.

## Current Stack

- The frontend is React + TypeScript under `gui/src/`
- Regular app UI lives in `gui/src/apps/<appname>/`
- Regular app backend code lives in `server/apps/<appname>/`
- System capabilities live in `server/main/`, for example chat, tasks, settings, memory, and files
- Shared backend helpers live in `server/shared/`

## Frontend Registration

- Desktop app registration lives in `gui/src/apps/index.ts`
- To add an openable desktop app, import its component there and add it to the `apps` array
- App definitions need at least `id`, `name`, `icon`, `component`, and `defaultDesktopWindowSize`
- Use `minDesktopWindowSize` when a window has a practical lower bound
- Secondary windows such as file viewers can be opened with `windowManager.openComponent(...)`

## Backend Registration

- Regular backend apps are registered in `server/apps/registry.ts`
- App modules default-export an object with `name`, `match(path)`, and `handleApi(req,res,path)`
- Apps with databases may also expose `initDb`
- Apps with background runtimes may also expose `initRuntime`
- `server/apps/index.ts` loads the registry, initializes apps, and routes `/apps/*` requests to the matching app

## API Rules

- Regular app endpoints use `/apps/<appname>/...`
- System endpoints use `/api/...`
- Prefer `GET` for reads and `POST` + JSON body for changes
- Read request bodies with `readBody(req)`
- Return JSON with `json(res, data, status?)`
- Return `false` from `handleApi` for unmatched app paths so the apps server can emit the 404

## Database Rules

- System tables live in `database/aios.db`
- App databases live in `database/apps/<app>.db`
- Create app databases with `createAppDb("<app>.db")` from `server/shared/apps/db/createAppDb.ts`
- Table creation usually belongs in `server/apps/<app>/repository/init.ts`
- Keep SQL access in focused `repository/` files and business logic in `service/`
- During development, do not preserve old schema compatibility unless explicitly required; maintain the current clean schema

## Language And Docs

- App documentation source lives in `language/<lang>/apps/<app>/APP.md`
- Startup baking generates `apps/<app>/APP.md` and mirrors it to existing `server/apps/<app>/APP.md`
- Prompt and long-lived UI text in source should use `__T_...__` tokens and be baked by `scripts/start.ts <lang> --force`
- Do not edit generated runtime docs under top-level `apps/` as the source of truth; edit language source files

## Reload Rules

- Changes under `gui/` need a frontend build or Vite refresh; when using reload, include `build: true`
- Changes under `server/apps/` need `restartApps: true`
- Changes under `server/main/` or `server/shared/` need `restartServer: true`
- The app creator usually calls `/api/runtime/reload/request` to notify the UI, then uses `/api/runtime/reload` or `/api/runtime/reload/test` as needed

## AI Execution Rules

- Read neighboring app implementations first, then place files in the same shape
- Update frontend, backend, registries, and language APP.md together so the feature is not half-wired
- `chat`, `tasks`, `settings`, `memory`, and `files` are system apps and should not be treated like ordinary `server/apps/` apps
- Persistent behavior changes belong in source code, not temporary shell patches or edits only in a runtime copy
